# `ECOTOXr`

[](https://github.com/pepijn-devries/ECOTOXr/) `ECOTOXr` can be used to
explore and analyse data from the [US EPA ECOTOX
database](https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/). More specifically you can:

- Build a local SQLite copy of the [US EPA ECOTOX
  database](https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/)
- Search and extract data from the local database
- Sanitise raw data from the local database
- Use experimental features to search the online dashboards:
  [ECOTOX](https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/search.cfm) and
  [CompTox](https://comptox.epa.gov/dashboard/batch-search)

## Why use `ECOTOXr`?

The `ECOTOXr` package allows you to search and extract data from the
[ECOTOXicological Knowledgebase](https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/) and
import it directly into `R`. This will allow you to formalize and
document the search- and extract-procedures in `R` code. This makes it
easier to share and reproduce such procedures and its results. As
illustrated in the graphical abstract below. Moreover, you can directly
apply any statistical analysis offered in `R`.

![From De Vries et al. (2024); Creative
commons](reference/figures/graphical-abstract.png)

From [De Vries et
al. (2024)](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chemosphere.2024.143078);
[Creative commons](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)

## Installation

> Get CRAN version

``` r

install.packages("ECOTOXr")
```

> Get development version from r-universe

``` r

install.packages("ECOTOXr", repos = c("https://pepijn-devries.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))
```

## Usage

### Preparing the database

Although `ECOTOXr` has experimental features to search the online
database. The package will reach its full potential when you build a
copy of the database on your local machine.

> Download and build a local copy of the latest ASCII export of the US
> EPA ECOTOX database

``` r

download_ecotox_data()
```

### Searching the local database for species and substances

Once built, you can search the local database for species and substances
using different strategies. You can use the build-in search function of
this package, or you can write custom queries using either the simple
query language (SQL) or `dplyr` verbs. More details in the following
vignettes:
[`vignette("searching-ecotox")`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/articles/searching-ecotox.md)
and
[`vignette("ecotox-schema")`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/articles/ecotox-schema.md).

## Disclaimers

It is the end-users own responsibility to check the quality of collected
data, using the original referenced source in order to evaluate its
fitness for use, see also:
<https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/help.cfm#info-limitations>.

Note that the package maintainer is not affiliated with the US EPA, this
package is therefore **not** official US EPA software.

## Code of Conduct

Please note that the ECOTOXr project is released with a [Contributor
Code of
Conduct](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.html).
By contributing to this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

## Resources

- De Vries, P. (2024) ECOTOXr: An R package for reproducible and
  transparent retrieval of data from EPA’s ECOTOX database.
  *Chemosphere* 364 143078
  <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chemosphere.2024.143078>
- [Manual of the CRAN
  release](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=ECOTOXr)
- EPA ECOTOX help <https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/help.cfm>
- Olker, Jennifer H.; Elonen, Colleen M.; Pilli, Anne; Anderson, Arne;
  Kinziger, Brian; Erickson, Stephen; Skopinski, Michael; Pomplun,
  Anita; LaLone, Carlie A.; Russom, Christine L.; Hoff, Dale. (2022):
  The ECOTOXicology Knowledgebase: A Curated Database of Ecologically
  Relevant Toxicity Tests to Support Environmental Research and Risk
  Assessment. *Environmental Toxicology and Chemistry* 41(6) 1520-1539
  <https://doi.org/10.1002/etc.5324>

# Package index

## All functions

- [`as_date_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/as_date_ecotox.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Values represented by ECOTOX `character` to dates

- [`as_numeric_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/as_numeric_ecotox.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Values represented by ECOTOX `character` to `numeric`

- [`as_unit_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/as_unit_ecotox.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Text from the ECOTOX database to `mixed_units`

- [`build_ecotox_sqlite()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/build_ecotox_sqlite.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Build an SQLite database from zip archived tables
  downloaded from EPA website

- [`cas()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`is.cas()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`as.cas()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`` `[[`( ``*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`` `[`( ``*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`` `[[<-`( ``*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`` `[<-`( ``*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`format(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`as.character(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`show.cas()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`print(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`as.list(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`as.double(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`as.integer(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`c(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  [`as.data.frame(`*`<cas>`*`)`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cas.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Functions for handling chemical abstract service
  (CAS) registry numbers

- [`check_ecotox_availability()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/check_ecotox_availability.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Check whether a ECOTOX database exists locally

- [`check_ecotox_build()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/check_ecotox_build.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Check the locally build database for validity

- [`check_ecotox_version()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/check_ecotox_version.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Check if the locally build database is up to date

- [`cite_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/cite_ecotox.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Cite the downloaded copy of the ECOTOX database

- [`dbConnectEcotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/dbConnectEcotox.md)
  [`dbDisconnectEcotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/dbConnectEcotox.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Open or close a connection to the local ECOTOX
  database

- [`download_ecotox_data()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/download_ecotox_data.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Download and extract ECOTOX database files and
  compose database

- [`get_ecotox_info()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/get_ecotox_info.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Get information on the local ECOTOX database when
  available

- [`get_ecotox_url()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/get_ecotox_url.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Get ECOTOX download URL from EPA website

- [`get_ecotox_sqlite_file()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/get_path.md)
  [`get_ecotox_path()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/get_path.md)
  **\[stable\]** : The local path to the ECOTOX database (directory or
  sqlite file)

- [`list_ecotox_fields()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/list_ecotox_fields.md)
  **\[stable\]** : List the field names that are available from the
  ECOTOX database

- [`mixed_to_single_unit()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/mixed_to_single_unit.md)
  : Convert mixed units to a specific unit

- [`process_ecotox_dates()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/process_ecotox_dates.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Process ECOTOX search results by converting `character` to dates where
  relevant

- [`process_ecotox_numerics()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/process_ecotox_numerics.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Process ECOTOX search results by converting `character` to `numeric`
  where relevant

- [`process_ecotox_units()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/process_ecotox_units.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Process ECOTOX search results by converting `character` to units where
  relevant

- [`search_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/search_ecotox.md)
  [`search_ecotox_lazy()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/search_ecotox.md)
  [`search_query_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/search_ecotox.md)
  **\[stable\]** : Search and retrieve toxicity records from the
  database

- [`websearch_ecotox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/websearch.md)
  [`list_ecotox_web_fields()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/websearch.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Search and retrieve toxicity records from the
  online database

- [`websearch_comptox()`](https://pepijn-devries.github.io/ECOTOXr/reference/websearch_comptox.md)
  **\[experimental\]** :

  Search and retrieve substance information from
  <https://comptox.epa.gov/dashboard>
